Identification.LongTitle: A second Act to implement certain provisions of the budget tabled in Parliament on March 22, 2017 and other measures

Provision.Text: Her Excellency the Governor General recommends to the House of Commons the appropriation of public revenue under the circumstances, in the manner and for the purposes set out in a measure entitled “A second Act to implement certain provisions of the budget tabled in Parliament on March 22, 2017 and other measures”.
Part 1 implements certain income tax measures proposed in the March 22, 2017 budget by

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: removing the classification of the costs of drilling a discovery well as “Canadian exploration expenses”;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: eliminating the ability for small oil and gas companies to reclassify up to $1 million of “Canadian development expenses” as “Canadian exploration expenses”;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: revising the anti-avoidance rules for registered education savings plans and registered disability savings plans;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: eliminating the use of billed-basis accounting by designated professionals;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: providing enhanced tax treatment for eligible geothermal energy equipment;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: extending the base erosion rules to foreign branches of Canadian insurers;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: clarifying who has factual control of a corporation for income tax purposes;

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: introducing an election that would allow taxpayers to mark to market their eligible derivatives;

Provision.Label: (i)
Provision.Text: introducing a specific anti-avoidance rule that targets straddle transactions;

Provision.Label: (j)
Provision.Text: allowing tax-deferred mergers of switch corporations into multiple mutual fund trusts and allowing tax-deferred mergers of segregated funds; and

Provision.Label: (k)
Provision.Text: enhancing the protection of ecologically sensitive land donated to conservation charities and broadening the types of donations permitted.
It also implements other income tax measures by

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: closing loopholes surrounding the capital gains exemption on the sale of a principal residence;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: providing additional authority for certain tax purposes to nurse practitioners;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: ensuring that qualifying farmers and fishers selling to agricultural and fisheries cooperatives are eligible for the small business deduction;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: extending the types of reverse takeover transactions to which the corporate acquisition of control rules apply;

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: improving the consistency of rules applicable for expenditures in respect of scientific research and experimental development;

Provision.Label: (f)
Provision.Text: ensuring that the taxable income of federal credit unions is allocated among provinces and territories using the same allocation formula as applicable to the taxable income of banks;

Provision.Label: (g)
Provision.Text: ensuring the appropriate application of Canada’s international tax rules; and

Provision.Label: (h)
Provision.Text: improving the accuracy and consistency of the income tax legislation and regulations.
Part 2 implements certain goods and services tax/harmonized sales tax (GST/HST) measures confirmed in the March 22, 2017 budget by

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: introducing clarifications and technical improvements to the GST/HST rules applicable to certain pension plans and financial institutions;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: revising the GST/HST rules applicable to pension plans so that they apply to pension plans that use master trusts or master corporations;

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: revising and modernizing the GST/HST drop shipment rules to enhance the effectiveness of these rules and introduce technical improvements;

Provision.Label: (d)
Provision.Text: clarifying the application of the GST/HST to supplies of municipal transit services to accommodate the modern ways in which those services are provided and paid for; and

Provision.Label: (e)
Provision.Text: introducing housekeeping amendments to improve the accuracy and consistency of the GST/HST legislation.
It also implements a GST/HST measure announced on September 8, 2017 by revising the timing requirements for GST/HST rebate applications by public service bodies.
Part 3 amends the Excise Act to ensure that beer made from concentrate on the premises where it is consumed is taxed in a manner that is consistent with other beer products.

Part 4 amends the Federal-Provincial Fiscal Arrangements Act to allow the Minister of Finance on behalf of the Government of Canada, with the approval of the Governor in Council, to enter into coordinated cannabis taxation agreements with provincial governments. It also amends that Act to make related amendments.
Part 5 enacts and amends several Acts in order to implement various measures.
Division 1 of Part 5 amends the Bretton Woods and Related Agreements Act to update and clarify certain powers of the Minister of Finance in relation to the Bretton Woods institutions.
Division 2 of Part 5 enacts the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank Agreement Act which provides the required authority for Canada to become a member of the Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank.

Division 3 of Part 5 provides for the transfer from the Minister of Finance to the Minister of Foreign Affairs of the responsibility for three international development financing agreements entered into between Her Majesty in Right of Canada and the International Finance Corporation.

Division 4 of Part 5 amends the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act to clarify the treatment of, and protections for, eligible financial contracts in a bank resolution process. It also makes consequential amendments to the Payment Clearing and Settlement Act.

Division 5 of Part 5 amends the Bank of Canada Act to specify that the Bank of Canada may make loans or advances to members of the Canadian Payments Association that are secured by real property or immovables situated in Canada and to allow such loans and advances to be secured by way of an assignment or transfer of a right, title or interest in real property or immovables situated in Canada. It also amends the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation Act to specify that the Bank of Canada and the Canada Deposit Insurance Corporation are exempt from stays even where obligations are secured by real property or immovables.

Division 6 of Part 5 amends the Payment Clearing and Settlement Act in order to expand and enhance the oversight powers of the Bank of Canada by further strengthening the Bank’s ability to identify and respond to risks to financial market infrastructures in a proactive and timely manner.

Division 7 of Part 5 amends the Northern Pipeline Act to permit the Northern Pipeline Agency to annually recover from any company with a certificate of public convenience and necessity issued under that Act an amount equal to the costs incurred by that Agency with respect to that company.
Division 8 of Part 5 amends the Canada Labour Code in order to, among other things,

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: provide employees with a right to request flexible work arrangements from their employers;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: provide employees with a family responsibility leave for a maximum of three days, a leave for victims of family violence for a maximum of ten days and a leave for traditional Aboriginal practices for a maximum of five days; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: modify certain provisions related to work schedules, overtime, annual vacation, general holidays and bereavement leave, in order to provide greater flexibility in work arrangements.

Division 9 of Part 5 amends the Economic Action Plan 2015 Act, No. 1 to repeal the paragraph 167(1.2)(b) of the Canada Labour Code that it enacts, and to amend the related regulation-making provisions accordingly.

Division 10 of Part 5 approves and implements the Canadian Free Trade Agreement entered into by the Government of Canada and the governments of each province and territory to reduce or eliminate barriers to the free movement of persons, goods, services and investments. It also makes related amendments to the Energy Efficiency Act in order to facilitate, with respect to energy-using products or classes of energy-using products, the harmonization of requirements set out in regulations with those of a jurisdiction. Finally, it makes consequential amendments to the Financial Administration Act, the Department of Public Works and Government Services Act and the Procurement Ombudsman Regulations and it repeals the Timber Marking Act and the Agreement on Internal Trade Implementation Act.
Division 11 of Part 5 amends the Judges Act

Provision.Label: (a)
Provision.Text: to allow for the payment of annuities, in certain circumstances, to judges and their survivors and children, other than by way of grant of the Governor in Council;

Provision.Label: (b)
Provision.Text: to authorize the payment of salaries to the new Associate Chief Justice of the Court of Queen’s Bench of Alberta; and

Provision.Label: (c)
Provision.Text: to change the title of “senior judge” to “chief justice” for the superior trial courts of the territories.
It also makes consequential amendments to other Acts.
Division 12 of Part 5 amends the Business Development Bank of Canada Act to increase the maximum amount of the paid-in capital of the Business Development Bank of Canada.

Division 13 of Part 5 amends the Financial Administration Act to authorize, in an increased number of cases, the entering into of contracts or other arrangements that provide for a payment if there is a sufficient balance to discharge any debt that will be due under them during the fiscal year in which they are entered into.
Her Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Budget Implementation Act, 2017, No. 2.

Heading.Label: PART 1
Heading.TitleText: Amendments to the Income Tax Act and to Related Legislation

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10(14) of the Income Tax Act is repealed.
Section 10 of the Act is amended by adding the following before subsection (15):
If paragraph 34(a) applies in computing a taxpayer’s income from a business for the last taxation year of the taxpayer that begins before March 22, 2017, then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of computing the income of the taxpayer from the business, at the end of the first taxation year that begins after March 21, 2017,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the cost of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be one-fifth of the amount of its cost determined without reference to this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the fair market value of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be one-fifth of the amount of its fair market value determined without reference to this paragraph;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of computing the income of the taxpayer from the business, at the end of the second taxation year that begins after March 21, 2017,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the cost of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be two-fifths of the amount of its cost determined without reference to this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the fair market value of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be two-fifths of the amount of its fair market value determined without reference to this paragraph;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of computing the income of the taxpayer from the business, at the end of the third taxation year that begins after March 21, 2017,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the cost of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be three-fifths of the amount of its cost determined without reference to this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the fair market value of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be three-fifths of the amount of its fair market value determined without reference to this paragraph; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of computing the income of the taxpayer from the business, at the end of the fourth taxation year that begins after March 21, 2017,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the cost of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be four-fifths of the amount of its cost determined without reference to this paragraph, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the fair market value of the taxpayer’s work in progress is deemed to be four-fifths of the amount of its fair market value determined without reference to this paragraph.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 10(14.1) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is repealed.
Subsections (1) and (3) come into force on January 1, 2024.
Subsection (2) applies to taxation years ending after March 21, 2017.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 10:

Subsection (4) applies to a taxpayer in respect of a taxation year and subsequent taxation years if the taxpayer elects to have subsection (4) apply to the taxpayer and has filed that election in prescribed form on or before its filing-due date for the taxation year.

The Minister may, on application by the taxpayer in prescribed form, grant permission to the taxpayer to revoke its election under subsection (1). The revocation applies to each taxation year of the taxpayer that begins after the day on which the taxpayer is notified in writing that the Minister concurs with the revocation, on such terms and conditions as are specified by the Minister.

Notwithstanding subsection (1), if a taxpayer has, under subsection (2), revoked an election, any subsequent election under subsection (1) shall result in subsection (4) applying to the taxpayer in respect of each taxation year that begins after the day on which the prescribed form in respect of the subsequent election is filed by the taxpayer.
If this subsection applies to a taxpayer in respect of a taxation year,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer is a financial institution (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)) in the taxation year, each eligible derivative held by the taxpayer at any time in the taxation year is, for the purpose of applying the provisions of this Act and with such modifications as the context requires, deemed to be mark-to-market property (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)) of the taxpayer for the taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in any other case, subsection (6) applies to the taxpayer in respect of each eligible derivative held by the taxpayer at the end of the taxation year.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of this section, an eligible derivative, of a taxpayer for a taxation year, means a swap agreement, a forward purchase or sale agreement, a forward rate agreement, a futures agreement, an option agreement or a similar agreement, held at any time in the taxation year by the taxpayer, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the agreement is not a capital property, a Canadian resource property, a foreign resource property or an obligation on account of capital of the taxpayer;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: either

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the taxpayer has produced audited financial statements prepared in accordance with generally accepted accounting principles in respect of the taxation year, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer has not produced audited financial statements described in subparagraph (i), the agreement has a readily ascertainable fair market value; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: where the agreement is held by a financial institution (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)), the agreement is not a tracking property (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)), other than an excluded property (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)), of the financial institution.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies to a taxpayer in respect of each eligible derivative held by the taxpayer at the end of a taxation year, for each eligible derivative held by the taxpayer at the end of the taxation year, the taxpayer is deemed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to have disposed of the eligible derivative immediately before the end of the year and received proceeds or paid an amount, as the case may be, equal to its fair market value at the time of disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to have reacquired, or reissued or renewed, the eligible derivative at the end of the year at an amount equal to the proceeds or the amount, as the case may be, determined under paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: If a taxpayer holds, at the beginning of its first taxation year in respect of which an election referred to in subsection (1) applies (in this subsection referred to as the “election year”), an eligible derivative and, in the taxation year immediately preceding the election year, the taxpayer did not compute its profit or loss in respect of that eligible derivative in accordance with a method of profit computation that produces a substantially similar effect to subsection (6), then

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the taxpayer is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to have disposed of the eligible derivative immediately before the beginning of the election year and received proceeds or paid an amount, as the case may be, equal to its fair market value at that time, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to have reacquired, or reissued or renewed, the eligible derivative at the beginning of the election year at an amount equal to the proceeds or the amount, as the case may be, determined under subparagraph (i);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the profit or loss that would arise (determined without reference to this paragraph) on the deemed disposition in subparagraph (a)(i)

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is deemed not to arise in the taxation year immediately preceding the election year, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: is deemed to arise in the taxation year in which the taxpayer disposes of the eligible derivative (otherwise than because of paragraphs (6)(a) or 142.5(2)(a)); and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: for the purpose of applying subsection 18(15) in respect of the disposition of the eligible derivative referred to in subparagraph (b)(ii), the profit or loss deemed to arise because of that subparagraph is included in determining the amount of the transferor’s loss, if any, from the disposition.

Subsection.Text: If subsection (4) does not apply to a taxpayer referred to in paragraph (4)(b) in respect of a taxation year, a method of profit computation that produces a substantially similar effect to subsection (6) shall not be used for the purpose of computing the taxpayer’s income from a business or property in respect of a swap agreement, a forward purchase or sale agreement, a forward rate agreement, a futures agreement, an option agreement or a similar agreement for the taxation year.
For the purposes of subsections (4) to (7), if an agreement that is an eligible derivative of a taxpayer is not a property of the taxpayer, the taxpayer is deemed

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to hold the eligible derivative at any time while the taxpayer is a party to the agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to have disposed of the eligible derivative when it is settled or extinguished in respect of the taxpayer.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to taxation years that begin after March 21, 2017.
Subsection 12(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (d.1):

Paragraph.Label: (d.2)
Paragraph.Text: any amount deducted under paragraph 20(1)(m.3) as a reserve in computing the taxpayer’s income for the immediately preceding taxation year;

Subsection.Text: Subparagraphs 12(1)(z.7)(i) and (ii) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer acquires a property under a derivative forward agreement in the year, the portion of the amount by which the fair market value of the property at the time it is acquired by the taxpayer exceeds the cost to the taxpayer of the property that is attributable to an underlying interest other than an underlying interest referred to in subparagraphs (b)(i) to (iii) of the definition derivative forward agreement in subsection 248(1), or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the taxpayer disposes of a property under a derivative forward agreement in the year, the portion of the amount by which the proceeds of disposition (within the meaning assigned by subdivision c) of the property exceeds the fair market value of the property at the time the agreement is entered into by the taxpayer that is attributable to an underlying interest other than an underlying interest referred to in clauses (c)(i)(A) to (C) of the definition derivative forward agreement in subsection 248(1).

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of bonds issued after 2000.
Subsection (2) applies to acquisitions and dispositions of property that occur after September 15, 2016.
Paragraph 18(12)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the conditions set out in subparagraph (a)(i) or (ii) are met, the amount for the work space that is deductible in computing the individual’s income for the year from the business shall not exceed the individual’s income for the year from the business, computed without reference to the amount and section 34.1; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 18(14)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the disposition is not a disposition that is deemed to have occurred by subsection 10.1(6) or (7), section 70, subsection 104(4), section 128.1, paragraph 132.2(3)(a) or (c) or subsection 138(11.3) or 149(10);

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 18(14)(c) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (2), is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the disposition is not a disposition that is deemed to have occurred by subsection 10.1(6) or (7), section 70, subsection 104(4), section 128.1, paragraph 132.2(3)(a) or (c) or subsection 138(11.3) or 138.2(4) or 149(10);

Subsection.Text: Section 18 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (16):
The following definitions apply in this subsection and subsections (18) to (23).

Definition.Text: offsetting position, in respect of a particular position of a person or partnership (in this definition referred to as the “holder”), means one or more positions that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: are held by

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the holder,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a person or partnership that does not deal at arm’s length with, or is affiliated with, the holder (in this subsection and subsections (20), (22) and (23) referred to as the “connected person”), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: for greater certainty, by any combination of the holder and one or more connected persons;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: have the effect, or would have the effect if each of the positions held by a connected person were held by the holder, of eliminating all or substantially all of the holder’s risk of loss and opportunity for gain or profit in respect of the particular position; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if held by a connected person, can reasonably be considered to have been held with the purpose of obtaining the effect described in paragraph (b). (position compensatoire)

Definition.Text: position, of a person or partnership, means one or more properties, obligations or liabilities of the person or partnership, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: each property, obligation or liability is

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a share in the capital stock of a corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest in a partnership,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest in a trust,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a commodity,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: foreign currency,

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: a swap agreement, a forward purchase or sale agreement, a forward rate agreement, a futures agreement, an option agreement or a similar agreement,

Subparagraph.Label: (vii)
Subparagraph.Text: a debt owed to or owing by the person or partnership that, at any time,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: is denominated in a foreign currency,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: would be described in paragraph 7000(1)(d) of the Income Tax Regulations if that paragraph were read without reference to the words “other than one described in paragraph (a), (b) or (c)”, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: is convertible into or exchangeable for an interest, or for civil law a right, in any property that is described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iv),

Subparagraph.Label: (viii)
Subparagraph.Text: an obligation to transfer or return to another person or partnership a property identical to a particular property described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii) that was previously transferred or lent to the person or partnership by that other person or partnership, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ix)
Subparagraph.Text: an interest, or for civil law a right, in any property that is described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (vii); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to conclude that, if there is more than one property, obligation or liability, each of them is held in connection with each other. (position)

Definition.Text: successor position, in respect of a position (in this definition referred to as the “initial position”), means a particular position if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the particular position is an offsetting position in respect of a second position;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the second position was an offsetting position in respect of the initial position that was disposed of at a particular time; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the particular position was entered into during the period that begins 30 days before, and ends 30 days after, the particular time. (position remplaçante)

Definition.Text: unrecognized loss, in respect of a position of a person or partnership at a particular time in a taxation year, means the loss, if any, that would be deductible in computing the income of the person or partnership for the year with respect to the position if it were disposed of immediately before the particular time at its fair market value at the time of disposition. (perte non constatée)

unrecognized profit, in respect of a position of a person or partnership at a particular time in a taxation year, means the profit, if any, that would be included in computing the income of the person or partnership for the year with respect to the position if it were disposed of immediately before the particular time at its fair market value at the time of disposition. (bénéfice non constaté)

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (20), subsection (19) applies in respect of a disposition of a particular position by a person or partnership (in this subsection and subsections (19), (20) and (22) referred to as the “transferor”), if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disposition is not a disposition that is deemed to have occurred by section 70, subsection 104(4), section 128.1 or subsection 138(11.3) or 149(10);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor is not a financial institution (as defined in subsection 142.2(1)), a mutual fund corporation or a mutual fund trust; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the particular position was, immediately before the disposition, not a capital property, or an obligation or liability on account of capital, of the transferor.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies in respect of a disposition of a particular position by a transferor, the portion of the transferor’s loss, if any, from the disposition of the particular position that is deductible in computing the transferor’s income for a particular taxation year is the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the particular taxation year is the taxation year in which the disposition occurs, the amount of the loss determined without reference to this subsection (which is, for greater certainty, subject to subsection (15)), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other taxation year, nil;

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the disposition occurred in a preceding taxation year, the amount determined for C in respect of the disposition for the immediately preceding taxation year, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: in any other case, nil; and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the lesser of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (a)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined for A for the taxation year in which the disposition occurs, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (b)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the amount determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of unrecognized profit at the end of the particular taxation year in respect of

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the particular position,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: positions that are offsetting positions in respect of the particular position (or would be, to the extent that there is no successor position in respect of the particular position, if the particular position continued to be held by the transferor),

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: successor positions in respect of the particular position (for this purpose, a successor position in respect of a position includes a successor position that is in respect of a successor position in respect of the position), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (iv)
FormulaParagraph.Text: positions that are offsetting positions in respect of any successor position referred to in subparagraph (iii) (or would be, if any such successor position continued to be held by the holder),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is the amount of unrecognized loss at the end of the particular taxation year in respect of positions referred to in subparagraphs (i) to (iv) of the description of D, and
is the total of all amounts each of which is an amount determined by the formula
is the amount determined for A for the taxation year in which the disposition occurs in respect of any position that was disposed of prior to the disposition of the particular position, if

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the particular position was a successor position in respect of that position (for this purpose, a successor position in respect of a position includes a successor position that is in respect of a successor position in respect of the position), and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: that position was

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an offsetting position in respect of the particular position,

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: an offsetting position in respect of a position in respect of which the particular position was a successor position (for this purpose, a successor position in respect of a position includes a successor position that is in respect of a successor position in respect of the position), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (C)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the particular position, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the total of all amounts each of which is, in respect of a position described in G, an amount determined under the first formula in this subsection for the particular taxation year or a preceding taxation year.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (19) does not apply in respect of a particular position of a transferor if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: it is the case that

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: either the particular position, or the offsetting position in respect of the particular position, consists of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: commodities that the holder of the position manufactures, produces, grows, extracts or processes, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: debt that the holder of the position incurs in the course of a business that consists of one or any combination of the activities described in clause (A), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that the position not described in subparagraph (i) — the particular position if the offsetting position is described in subparagraph (i) or the offsetting position if the particular position is described in that subparagraph — is held to reduce the risk, with respect to the position described in subparagraph (i), from

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: in the case of a position described in clause (i)(A), price changes or fluctuations in the value of currency with respect to the goods described in clause (i)(A), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: in the case of a position described in clause (i)(B), fluctuations in interest rates or in the value of currency with respect to the debt described in clause (i)(B);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the transferor or a connected person (in this paragraph referred to as the “holder”) continues to hold a position — that would be an offsetting position in respect of the particular position if the particular position continued to be held by the transferor — throughout a 30-day period beginning on the date of disposition of the particular position, and at no time during the period

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: is the holder’s risk of loss or opportunity for gain or profit with respect to the position reduced in any material respect by another position entered into or disposed of by the holder, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: would the holder’s risk of loss or opportunity for gain or profit with respect to the position be reduced in any material respect by another position entered into or disposed of by a connected person, if the other position were entered into or disposed of by the holder; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that none of the main purposes of the series of transactions or events, or any of the transactions or events in the series, of which the holding of both the particular position and offsetting position are part, is to avoid, reduce or defer tax that would otherwise be payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of subsections (17) to (23),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if a position of a person or partnership is not a property of the person or partnership, the person or partnership is deemed

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to hold the position at any time while it is a position of the person or partnership, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to have disposed of the position when the position is settled or extinguished in respect of the person or partnership;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a disposition of a position is deemed to include a disposition of a portion of the position;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a position held by one or more persons or partnerships referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition offsetting position in subsection (17) is deemed to be an offsetting position in respect of a particular position of a person or partnership if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: there is a high degree of negative correlation between changes in value of the position and the particular position, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that the principal purpose of the series of transactions or events, or any of the transactions in the series, of which the holding of both the position and the particular position are part, is to avoid, reduce or defer tax that would otherwise be payable under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one or more positions held by one or more persons or partnerships referred to in paragraph (a) of the definition offsetting position in subsection (17) are deemed to be a successor position in respect of a particular position of a person or partnership if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: a portion of the particular position was disposed of at a particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the position is, or the positions include, as the case may be, a position that consists of the portion of the particular position that was not disposed of (in this paragraph referred to as the “remaining portion of the particular position”),

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: where there is more than one position, the position or positions that do not consist of the remaining portion of the particular position were entered into during the period that begins 30 days before, and ends 30 days after, the particular time,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the position is, or the positions taken together would be, as the case may be, an offsetting position in respect of a second position (within the meaning of the definition successor position in subsection (17)),

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the second position was an offsetting position in respect of the particular position, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: it can reasonably be considered that the principal purpose of the series of transactions or events, or any of the transactions in the series, of which the disposition of a portion of the particular position and the holding of one or more positions are part, is to avoid, reduce or defer tax that would otherwise be payable under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (23) applies if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at any time in a particular taxation year of a transferor, a position referred to in any of subparagraphs (ii) to (iv) of the description of D in subsection (19) (in this subsection and subsection (23) referred to as the “gain position”) is held by a connected person;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the connected person disposes of the gain position in the particular taxation year; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the taxation year of the connected person in which the disposition referred to in paragraph (b) occurs ends after the end of the particular taxation year.

Subsection.Text: If this subsection applies, for the purposes of the definition unrecognized profit in subsection (17) and subsection (19), the portion of the profit, if any, realized from the disposition of the gain position referred to in paragraph (22)(b) that is determined by the following formula is deemed to be unrecognized profit in respect of the gain position until the end of the taxation year of the connected person in which the disposition occurs:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount of the profit otherwise determined;
is the number of days in the taxation year of the connected person in which the disposition referred to in paragraph (22)(b) occurs that are after the end of the particular taxation year; and
is the total number of days in the taxation year of the connected person in which the disposition referred to in paragraph (22)(b) occurs.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies to the 2011 and subsequent taxation years.
Subsection (2) applies to taxation years that begin after March 21, 2017.
Subsection (3) applies to taxation years that begin after 2017.
Subsection (4) applies in respect of a position (as defined in subsection 18(17) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4)) of a person or partnership if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the position is acquired, entered into, renewed or extended, or becomes owing, by the person or partnership after March 21, 2017; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an offsetting position (as defined in subsection 18(17) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4)) in respect of the position is acquired, entered into, renewed or extended, or becomes owing, by the person or partnership or a connected person (within the meaning of subsection 18(17) of the Act, as enacted by subsection (4)) after March 21, 2017.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 20(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following after paragraph (m.2):

Paragraph.Label: (m.3)
Paragraph.Text: the unamortized amount at the end of the year in respect of the amount that was received in excess of the principal amount of a bond (in this paragraph referred to as the “premium”) received by the issuer in the year, or a previous year, for issuing the bond (in this paragraph referred to as the “new bond”) if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the terms of the new bond are identical to the terms of bonds previously issued by the taxpayer (in this paragraph referred to as the “old bonds”), except for the date of issuance and total principal amount of the bonds,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the old bonds were part of an issuance (in this paragraph referred to as the “original issuance”) of bonds by the taxpayer,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the interest rate on the old bonds was reasonable at the time of the original issuance,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the new bond is issued on the re-opening of the original issuance,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the premium at the time of issuance of the new bond is reasonable, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of the premium has been included in the taxpayer’s income for the year or a previous taxation year;

Subsection.Text: Clauses (i)(A) and (B) of the description of A in paragraph 20(1)(xx) of the Act are replaced by the following:

FormulaParagraph.Label: (A)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer acquires a property under the agreement in the year or a preceding taxation year, the portion of the amount by which the cost to the taxpayer of the property exceeds the fair market value of the property at the time it is acquired by the taxpayer that is attributable to an underlying interest other than an underlying interest referred to in subparagraphs (b)(i) to (iii) of the definition derivative forward agreement in subsection 248(1), or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (B)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer disposes of a property under the agreement in the year or a preceding taxation year, the portion of the amount by which the fair market value of the property at the time the agreement is entered into by the taxpayer exceeds the proceeds of disposition (within the meaning assigned by subdivision c) of the property that is attributable to an underlying interest other than an underlying interest referred to in clauses (c)(i)(A) to (C) of the definition derivative forward agreement in subsection 248(1), and

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of bonds issued after 2000.
Subsection (2) applies in respect of acquisitions and dispositions of property that occur after September 15, 2016.
Paragraph 34(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: if the taxpayer so elects in the taxpayer’s return of income under this Part for the year and the year begins before March 22, 2017, there shall not be included any amount in respect of work in progress at the end of the year; and

Subsection.Text: Section 34 of the Act, as amended by subsection (1), is repealed.
Subsection (1) applies to taxation years ending after March 21, 2017.
Subsection (2) comes into force on January 1, 2024.
Subclause 37(8)(a)(ii)(B)(II) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: an expenditure of a current nature for the prosecution of scientific research and experimental development in Canada directly undertaken on behalf of the taxpayer,

Subsection.Text: Subsection 37(11) of the Act is replaced by the following:

A prescribed form must be filed by a taxpayer with the Minister in respect of any expenditure, that would be incurred by the taxpayer in a taxation year that begins after 1995 if this Act were read without reference to subsection 78(4), that is claimed by the taxpayer for the year as a deduction under this section, on or before the day that is 12 months after the taxpayer’s filing due-date for the taxation year, containing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: prescribed information in respect of the expenditure; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: claim preparer information, as defined in subsection 162(5.3).

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (12), if the prescribed information in respect of an expenditure referred to in paragraph (11)(a) is not contained in the form referred to in subsection (11), no amount in respect of the expenditure may be deducted under subsection (1).
Subsection (1) applies in respect of expenditures incurred after September 16, 2016.
Clause 39(1)(c)(iv)(B) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a bankrupt that was a small business corporation at the time it last became a bankrupt, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 39(2.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

If at any time a corporation resident in Canada or a partnership of which such a corporation is a member (such corporation or partnership referred to in this subsection and subsections (2.2) and (2.3) as the “borrowing party”) has received a loan from, or become indebted to, a creditor that is a foreign affiliate (referred to in this subsection and subsections (2.2) and (2.3) as a “creditor affiliate”) of a qualifying entity, or that is a partnership (referred to in this subsection and subsection (2.3) as a “creditor partnership”) of which such an affiliate is a member, and the loan or indebtedness is at a later time repaid, in whole or in part, then the amount of the borrowing party’s capital gain or capital loss determined, in the absence of this subsection, under subsection (2) in respect of the repayment, is to be reduced

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a capital gain

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the creditor is a creditor affiliate, by an amount, not exceeding that capital gain, that is equal to twice the amount that would — in the absence of subparagraph 40(2)(g)(ii) and paragraph 95(2)(g.04) and on the assumption that the creditor affiliate’s capital loss in respect of the repayment of the loan or indebtedness were a capital gain of the creditor affiliate, the creditor affiliate had no other income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year, and no other foreign affiliate of a qualifying entity had any income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year — be the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that would be included in computing a qualifying entity’s income under subsection 91(1) for its taxation year that includes the last day of the taxation year of the creditor affiliate that includes the later time, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the creditor is a creditor partnership, by an amount, not exceeding that capital gain, that is equal to twice the amount that is the total of each amount, determined in respect of a particular member of the creditor partnership that is a foreign affiliate of a qualifying entity, that would — in the absence of subparagraph 40(2)(g)(ii) and paragraph 95(2)(g.04) and on the assumption that the creditor partnership’s capital loss in respect of the repayment of the loan or indebtedness were a capital gain of the creditor partnership, the particular member had no other income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year, and no other foreign affiliate of a qualifying entity had any income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year — be the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that would be included in computing a qualifying entity’s income under subsection 91(1) for its taxation year that includes the last day of the taxation year of the particular member that includes the last day of the creditor partnership’s fiscal period that includes that later time; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a capital loss

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: if the creditor is a creditor affiliate, by an amount, not exceeding that capital loss, that is equal to twice the amount, in respect of the creditor affiliate’s capital gain in respect of the repayment of the loan or indebtedness, that would — in the absence of paragraph 95(2)(g.04) and on the assumption that the creditor affiliate had no other income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year, and no other foreign affiliate of a qualifying entity had any income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year — be the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that would be included in computing a qualifying entity’s income under subsection 91(1) for its taxation year that includes the last day of the taxation year of the creditor affiliate that includes the later time, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the creditor is a creditor partnership, by an amount, not exceeding that capital loss, that is equal to twice the amount, in respect of the creditor partnership’s capital gain in respect of the repayment of the loan or indebtedness, that is the total of each amount, determined in respect of a particular member of the creditor partnership that is a foreign affiliate of a qualifying entity, that would — in the absence of paragraph 95(2)(g.04) and on the assumption that the particular member had no other income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year, and no other foreign affiliate of a qualifying entity had any income, loss, capital gain or capital loss for any taxation year — be the total of all amounts each of which is an amount that would be included in computing a qualifying entity’s income under subsection 91(1) for its taxation year that includes the last day of the taxation year of the particular member that includes the last day of the creditor partnership’s fiscal period that includes the later time.

Subsection.Text: For purposes of subsections (2.1) and (2.3), qualifying entity means

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a borrowing party that is a corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the borrowing party,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation resident in Canada of which

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the borrowing party is a subsidiary wholly-owned corporation, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a corporation described in this paragraph is a subsidiary wholly-owned corporation,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a corporation resident in Canada

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: each share of the capital stock of which is owned by

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: the borrowing party, or

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: a corporation that is described in this subparagraph or subparagraph (ii), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: all or substantially all of the capital stock of which is owned by one or more corporations resident in Canada that are borrowing parties in respect of the creditor affiliate because of subsection 90(7), or

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: a partnership each member of which is

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a corporation described in any of subparagraphs (i) to (iii), or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: another partnership described in this subparagraph; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a borrowing party that is a partnership,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the borrowing party,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if each member — determined as if each member of a partnership that is a member of another partnership is a member of that other partnership — of the borrowing party is either a particular corporation resident in Canada (in this paragraph referred to as the “parent”) or a corporation resident in Canada that is a subsidiary wholly-owned corporation, as defined in subsection 87(1.4), of the parent,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the parent, or

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a corporation resident in Canada that is a subsidiary wholly-owned corporation, as defined in subsection 87(1.4), of the parent, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: a partnership each member of which is any of

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: the borrowing party,

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a corporation described in subparagraph (ii), and

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: another partnership described in this subparagraph.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2.1) and paragraph 95(2)(g.04) do not apply in respect of a repayment, in whole or in part, of a loan or indebtedness if an election has been filed with the Minister before 2019 jointly by

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the borrowing party;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if the creditor is a creditor affiliate, each qualifying entity of which the creditor affiliate is a foreign affiliate; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the creditor is a creditor partnership, each qualifying entity of which a member of the creditor partnership is a foreign affiliate.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of bankruptcies that occur after April 26, 1995.

Subsection (2) applies in respect of portions of loans received and indebtedness incurred before August 20, 2011 that remain outstanding on August 19, 2011 and that are repaid, in whole or in part, before August 20, 2016.
The description of B in paragraph 40(2)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

FormulaDefinition.Text: is

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if the taxpayer was resident in Canada during the year that includes the acquisition date, one plus the number of taxation years that end after the acquisition date for which the property is the taxpayer’s principal residence and during which the taxpayer was resident in Canada, or

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: if it is not the case that the taxpayer was resident in Canada during the year that includes the acquisition date, the number of taxation years that end after the acquisition date for which the property was the taxpayer’s principal residence and during which the taxpayer was resident in Canada,

Subsection.Text: Paragraphs 40(3)(d) and (e) of the Act are replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of section 93 and subsections 116(6) and (6.1), the property is deemed to have been disposed of by the taxpayer at that time, and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 2(3) and sections 110.6 and 150, the property is deemed to have been disposed of by the taxpayer in the year.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 40(3.1)(b) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the purposes of subsection 2(3), section 110.6, subsections 116(6) and (6.1) and section 150, the interest is deemed to have been disposed of by the member at that time.

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 40(6) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Principal residence — property owned at end of 1981
Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (6.1), if a property was owned by a taxpayer, whether jointly with another person or otherwise, at the end of 1981 and continuously from the beginning of 1982 until disposed of by the taxpayer, the amount of the gain determined under paragraph (2)(b) in respect of the disposition shall not exceed the amount, if any, by which the total of
Section 40 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (6):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Principal residence — property owned at end of 2016
Subsection.Text: If a trust owns property at the end of 2016, the trust is not in its first taxation year that begins after 2016 a trust described in subparagraph (c.1)(iii.1) of the definition principal residence in section 54, the trust disposes of the property after 2016, the disposition is the trust’s first disposition of the property after 2016 and the trust owns the property, whether jointly with another person or otherwise, continuously from the beginning of 2017 until the disposition,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection (6) does not apply to the disposition; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the trust’s gain determined under paragraph (2)(b) in respect of the disposition is the amount, if any, determined by the formula

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the trust’s gain calculated in accordance with paragraph (2)(b) on the assumption that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the trust disposed of the property on December 31, 2016 for proceeds of disposition equal to its fair market value on that date, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: paragraph (a) did not apply in respect of the disposition described in subparagraph (i),

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the trust’s gain in respect of the disposition calculated in accordance with paragraph (2)(b) on the assumption that

FormulaParagraph.Label: (i)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the description of B in that paragraph is read without reference to “one plus”, and

FormulaParagraph.Label: (ii)
FormulaParagraph.Text: the trust acquired the property on January 1, 2017 at a cost equal to its fair market value on December 31, 2016, and

FormulaDefinition.Text: is the amount, if any, by which the fair market value of the property on December 31, 2016 exceeds the proceeds of disposition of the property determined without reference to this subsection.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of dispositions that occur after October 2, 2016.
Subsections (2) and (3) apply in respect of gains from dispositions that occur after September 15, 2016.
The portion of subsection 43(2) of the Act before the formula in paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of subsection (1) and section 53, if at any time a taxpayer disposes of a covenant or an easement to which land is subject or, in the case of land in the Province of Quebec, a real or personal servitude, in circumstances where subsection 110.1(5) or 118.1(12) applies,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of the adjusted cost base to the taxpayer of the land immediately before the disposition that can reasonably be regarded as attributable to the covenant, easement or servitude, as the case may be, is deemed to be equal to the amount determined by the formula

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) applies in respect of gifts made after March 21, 2017.
Clause 53(2)(c)(i)(C) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: subsections 100(4), 112(3.1), (4), (4.2) as it read in its application to dispositions of property that occurred before April 27, 1995 and (5.2),

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) is deemed to have come into force on September 16, 2016.
Paragraph (c.1) of the definition principal residence in section 54 of the Act is amended by striking out “and” at the end of subparagraph (iii) and by adding the following after that subparagraph:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii.1)
Subparagraph.Text: if the year begins after 2016, the trust is, in the year,

Clause.Label: (A)
Clause.Text: a trust

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: for which a day is to be determined under paragraph 104(4)(a), (a.1) or (a.4) by reference to the death or later death, as the case may be, that has not occurred before the beginning of the year, of an individual who is resident in Canada during the year, and

Subclause.Label: (II)

Clause.Label: (B)
Clause.Text: a trust

Subclause.Label: (I)
Subclause.Text: that is a qualified disability trust (as defined in subsection 122(3)) for the year, and

Subclause.Label: (II)
Subclause.Text: an electing beneficiary (in this clause, as defined in subsection 122(3)) of which for the year is

Subsubclause.Text: resident in Canada during the year,
a specified beneficiary of the trust for the year, and
a spouse, common-law partner, former spouse or common-law partner or child of the settlor (in this subparagraph, as defined in subsection 108(1)) of the trust, or

Clause.Label: (C)
Clause.Text: a trust

who has not attained 18 years of age before the end of the year, and
a mother or father of whom is a settlor of the trust, and

the trust arose before the beginning of the year on and as a consequence of the death of a mother or father of the individual referred to in subclause (I), and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph (c) of the definition superficial loss in section 54 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a disposition deemed to have been made by subsection 45(1), section 48 as it read in its application before 1993, section 50 or 70, subsection 104(4), section 128.1, paragraph 132.2(3)(a) or (c), subsection 138(11.3) or 138.2(4) or 142.5(2), section 142.6 or any of subsections 144(4.1) and (4.2) and 149(10),

Subsection.Text: Subsection (2) applies to taxation years that begin after 2017.
Paragraph 56(1)(z.3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (z.3)
Paragraph.Text: any amount required by section 147.5 to be included in computing the taxpayer’s income for the year other than an amount distributed under a PRPP as a return of all or a portion of a contribution to the plan to the extent that the amount

Subparagraph.Text: is a payment described under clause 147.5(3)(d)(ii)(A) or (B), and
